text:
The is a figure of Eldian mythology who made a deal with Ymir Fritz, granting her the power of the Titans. The Subjects of Ymir are called the because of this belief.

The world was convinced that the Devil of All Earth was real, slain by the Marleyan hero Helos during the Great Titan War, until Willy Tybur revealed that the story of Helos is mere propaganda. Eren Kruger suspected that Ymir's deal with the Devil was fabricated by the enemies of her people, although the tale is told in an Eldian book as well.

Appearance

In all illustrations depicting the Devil, it is shown as a large, crouching figure with a menacing smile. In the book Frieda Reiss reads to Historia, the Devil is covered in a large cloak and hood, whereas in the book Jaeger reads to Grisha, its head is exposed, revealing two goat-like horns and a long tail. It also appears with sharp claws, large muscles, and pointed ears.

In a bronze statue in Liberio depicting Helos slaying the Devil, he takes on a much less demonic appearance, becoming more Titan-like with a thick beard and a full head of hair, lacking visible horns.

History

Over 1,800 years before the Fall of Wall Maria, a girl by the name of Ymir Fritz is said to have met with the Devil, and the two made a deal. It is unknown what Ymir offered the Devil according to the legends, but in return it granted her immense power, turning her into the first of all Titans. 

After the fall of the Eldian Empire during the Great Titan War, the newly-freed Marleyans in rule utilized this legend in order to demonize the Subjects of Ymir, who were already despised by most peoples of the world. In the following century, this narrative, on top of the countless atrocities committed by Eldia with the power of the Titans against other nations and peoples for millenia, would be used to constantly antagonize the Eldian populations, justifying their oppression and the need to one day exterminate all of them.

Eren Kruger later claimed to Grisha Jaeger that this myth was open to interpretation and that there was no absolute truth in the world, also giving the option that she obtained her powers by coming into contact with the source of all living matter.

At the time when Historia Reiss, a direct descendant of Ymir Fritz, was growing up on her family's farm in Wall Sina, her half-sister Frieda Reiss visited her frequently, teaching her important life lessons such as reading and writing. Occasionally, Frieda would teach Historia the story of Ymir and the Devil; however, in this story, the girl is named "Christa" instead of Ymir. Frieda instructed Historia to be a loving and beloved girl as "Christa" was.

Legacy

Return to Shiganshina arc

In the year 850, when the Scout Regiment discover Grisha Jaeger's basement, they find three journals. Within Grisha's writings, he recounts his childhood, when he was told the story of Ymir and her deal with the Devil by his father and Eren Kruger.

Marley arc

During the Tybur family's visit to Liberio, Willy Tybur visits a Marleyan estate to see the statue of Helos slaying the Devil.

War for Paradis arc

When Eren Jaeger enacted the Rumbling and reached Marley, the heat of the Wall Titans melted down the statue of Helos slaying the Devil as they trampled through Liberio.
